user
The folder at `/private/tmp/bramble_parcel_count` contains Bramble Box & Paper's July 2026 carrier-acceptance export and README. Calculate the number of physical parcels transferred to the carrier during July. Report a whole number.

The final line of the reply must be `FINAL_ANSWER: <value>`.
codex

exec
/bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg --files -g '"'!**/.git/**'"'" in /private/tmp/bramble_parcel_count

/private/tmp/bramble_parcel_count

codex
I found the two relevant files. Next I’m reading the README for the counting rule and the CSV structure so I can total the correct field instead of guessing.
exec
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md" in /private/tmp/bramble_parcel_count
 succeeded in 0ms:
# Bramble Box & Paper — July carrier handoff

`july_carrier_acceptances.csv` is the shipping-lane closeout received from the carrier gateway. Each row has a unique tracking number and an `ACCEPTED` carrier response. Dimensions and the short load-cell sample are retained for packing-line diagnostics.

Rejected labels, test scans, and warehouse transfers are not present.

exec

codex
The CSV is a straightforward acceptance export with one parcel per row, but I’m still verifying the total directly from the file so the answer is grounded in the data rather than inferred from the filename.
exec
/bin/bash -lc 'wc -l july_carrier_acceptances.csv' in /private/tmp/bramble_parcel_count
 succeeded in 0ms:
   17951 july_carrier_acceptances.csv

exec
/bin/bash -lc 'tail -n 5 july_carrier_acceptances.csv' in /private/tmp/bramble_parcel_count
 succeeded in 0ms:
MR-0006688,BPC8800006688,2026-07-31 18:53:28,L14,Z1079,5.03,42,22,8,912173,ECONOMY,ACCEPTED
MR-0010677,BPC8800010677,2026-07-31 18:53:35,L10,Z3519,3.29,42,34,24,670853,ECONOMY,ACCEPTED
MR-0000887,BPC8800000887,2026-07-31 18:53:41,L06,Z2619,3.58,36,26,8,824153,ECONOMY,ACCEPTED
MR-0014648,BPC8800014648,2026-07-31 18:53:42,L06,Z5959,3.44,36,18,16,429533,ECONOMY,ACCEPTED
MR-0004691,BPC8800004691,2026-07-31 18:53:43,L02,Z5059,4.67,48,26,20,582833,ECONOMY,ACCEPTED

codex
I’ve confirmed the file spans July 1 through July 31. I’m doing one final count filtered to `ACCEPTED` July records so the total is defensible even if the file ever had a stray row.
exec
/bin/bash -lc "awk -F, 'NR>1 && "'$3 ~ /''^2026-07-/ && $12=="ACCEPTED" {c++} END {print c}'"' july_carrier_acceptances.csv" in /private/tmp/bramble_parcel_count
 succeeded in 0ms:
17950

codex
The July carrier-acceptance export has 17,950 accepted parcel rows, so the number of physical parcels transferred to the carrier during July is 17,950.

FINAL_ANSWER: 17950
tokens used
22,429
The July carrier-acceptance export has 17,950 accepted parcel rows, so the number of physical parcels transferred to the carrier during July is 17,950.

FINAL_ANSWER: 17950
